evidence.snippet: The Silver Atena Universal FCC (Flight Control Computer) is a powerful avionics computer for the highly automated control of manned and unmanned small aircraft, drones, and helicopters.
*   UAVs / drones, OPVs, and RPAS

items.notes: FCC explicitly developed for UAVs/drones/helicopters; listed under Military Aviation nav as 'Flight Control Computer'

evidence.snippet: Whether for urban air mobility, drones, or hybrid flight concepts – we deliver scalable, air-cooled solutions with up to 50 kW of power.

Silver Atena's aero drive inverters are the ideal solution for electric propulsion systems in aviation – for example, for flight propulsion systems for eVTOLs (electric vertical take-off and landing) or servo motors for wing adjustment.
*   UAVs / Drones

items.notes: Electric FAN (motor + integrated SiC inverter) and FAN Inverter for UAVs/eVTOLs; FADEC for military aviation including drones

evidence.snippet: Silver Atena develops electric drives designed without compromise for propeller operation in aircraft. They combine zero emissions, minimal maintenance, and maximum efficiency.
*   UAVs – for maximum efficiency and low maintenance

items.notes: Electric Propeller Drive includes electric motor for propellers; also for UAVs

items.name: FADEC
items.product_type: Full Authority Digital Engine Control for aircraft turbines; dual-use civil/military including drones

evidence.snippet: Full Authority Digital Engine Control Unit (FADEC)
The dual-use capable Full Authority Digital Engine Control (FADEC) is a state-of-the-art, computer-controlled system to control all aspects of aircraft engine performance.

products.notes: Listed drone-relevant products only; automotive products (Traction Inverter, Battery Management Unit, etc.) omitted as not UXV-specific

evidence.snippet: We develop products with a clear focus on industrialisation and readiness for series production.
*   Series products from our own production sites

readiness.notes: Company operates own production sites and delivers series components; certified IATF 16949 and EN 9100; drone subsystem products have detailed specs sheets suggesting production readiness

tagline: Munich-based developer of flight-control computers, FADEC systems, and SiC-based power electronics (10–600 kW inverters, DC/DC converters, battery management) for aerospace, eVTOL, and defence platforms. Production-ready across avionics, electric propulsion, and ruggedized ground-control displays. NDAA and Blue UAS certification status unknown; public evidence of specific defense primes or US programs limited.
